content:
# Tmax

**Summary**: The temperature at which the maximum rate of hydrocarbon generation occurs.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] measured TMAX as a thermal maturity indicator.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] TMAX is used as a primary indicator for thermal maturity.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] measured during the pyrolysis run at the S2 peak

## Key Findings Across Sources

- TMAX values were also affected by preservation due to changes in S1 and S2 values. ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023, p. 13]])
- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] Core 1: 465-470°C; Core 2: 465-470°C; Core 3: 455-460°C.
